reviews.review_text:
[h1]Review of Killing Floor 2[/h1]
I've been a lover of wave based survival / horde shooter games for many years of my life. Killing Floor 2 has been one that's brought me some of the most fun thus far. Which is surprising to me, because I vividly remember playing it for the first time in 2017, but not really liking it. Until I was brought back into it a year later, and suddenly everything just started clicking positively for me.

KF2 was my primary introduction to the series. Previously, I had played a very limited amount of the first game, but I'll take another look at that some time in the future. I feel as though Killing Floor 2 is a great entry point for anyone who's interested in either the series itself, or a fun Co-Op horde shooter. 

[u]Blut, immer blut[/u]
These games do have a narrative that runs in the background. Mostly told through bits and pieces of dialogue, and the levels themselves. However, my focus has almost always been on the gameplay, as that takes center stage. If the game's lore is of interest to you though, there's plenty to read about. Horzine's involvement, the experiments, super soldiers, and ultimately how we're not stuck in this apocalyptic situation. The tone of Killing Floor 2 is sort of a mix between serious, and comedic. Cheesy one liners while you're running about blasting Zeds into mist, and the way certain boss enemies will taunt you. 

The addicting loop of the game stems from its wave based "Survival" mode. Being a game of this type, it's extremely important that the loop facilitate replayability, and if possible, provide a dynamic structure to its gameplay. This was the first thing that got me invested into KF2. Every time you play, waves are never the same. Exactly what Zeds spawn, where, and in what quantities, is always randomized. The only predictable stages are the boss waves, but even then there's a pool of different bosses the game chooses from. Now through in the wide variety of different maps, large selection of classes (Called Perks), multiple difficulties, various secondary objectives that can pop up during a round, and you're always in for something that differs from the last attempt. Survival mode sees you trying to make it through the final wave, depending on your choice of length (Short, Normal, Long). 

Survival isn't the only mode here though. There's also "Endless". Which is exactly as it sounds. This is probably where I tend to spend most of my time these days. It goes on virtually forever, with a slight increase in difficulty as the waves progress. As an added bonus though, Endless introduces modifiers/mutations that can appear randomly. Such as putting you through a wave of only bloats or scrakes, giving Zeds ridiculously sized heads, making Zeds volatile so they explode on death, and many more. All while your friendly neighborhood Patriarch commentates your progress.

Lastly, there's the somewhat newer "Objective" mode. This is the closest you'll get to a form of story gameplay. These have you following orders through a map, protecting zones, then moving to the next objective, until you reach the finale and successfully finish the mission. 

Fit with a fantastic blood pumping OST, and great visuals (Specifically the weapon animations). Throw in modding support, and there's even more to play around with.

I feel it's important to mention that although it is designed as a Co-Op game, it does allow for solo play. The more players that join for Co-Op, the higher the waves scale in difficulty & Zed count. Solo players are capable of enjoying the entire game on their own, including the hardest difficult; Hell on Earth.

[u]Choices, Progression, & Dosh[/u]
There's a lot of choice in how you take to defeating Zeds in the bloodiest way possible. KF2's classes, which are called "Perks", come in 10 different varieties. The unique aspect of them, is that each class corresponds to a specific weapon type & specialization (Providing unique buffs for self or party). SWAT, for example, uses primarily submachine guns & is built for clearly groups of weaker Zeds quickly. While something like Sharpshooter wields mostly large medium to long range precision rifles that do insane damage. Great for taking down elites or bosses. Now you can use any weapon on any class, but you would efficiently earn EXP while doing so, or gain any of the buffs that might apply to your weaponry too.

Perks can all be individually leveled up. The max being 25 per. Every 5 levels grants you a special point that you can use to choose between two perk skills per tier. Once you've maxed a perk, it can optionally be prestiged. Unlocking a new fancy badge for it, letting you start the grind all over with a permanent EXP bonus, and giving you plenty of additional playtime.

Dosh is earned from blasting away Zeds and completing optional objectives. It's used to buy any number of weapons, body armor, grenades, etc. It can also be shared amongst on members of your team.

There's also many different characters to pick and choose from. They have no effect on gameplay, but can be fully customized with a huge amount of unlockable.. and purchasable.. cosmetics. A lot of cosmetics, such as pieces for your character, or paints for your weaponry, can be unlocked through general play with a random drop chance. Some can be crafted. Others can be obtained from opening crates, but unfortunately a lot of the cosmetics are locked behind paywalls & RNG. Such as needing to buy keys to open crates, just for a chance to get the thing you want. Or hope someone's selling it on the market for a reasonable price. 

[u]I can't see 'em, but I smell 'em. (Complaints)[/u]
Thankfully, my gripes with the game are relatively minor. The biggest issue I have with KF2 is the way that Zeds spawn. There's a huge amount of entry points in maps, like vents or holes on the boundaries, where they often come from. The issue is that.. they also just spawn out of thin air if you don't have line of sight. The amount of times I've started a wave, and spun the camera around to see a group of Zeds pop into existence two feet off the ground, or rounded a corner and watching a group suddenly appear, is frustrating. It also makes defending, or creating choke points, difficult. Because no matter way, if you stand still for two seconds, you'll get jumped from behind.

It takes a lot of time to get use to the lack of a meaningful sprint. Every perk has different movement speed, so trying to sprint and watching your character move at a snail's pace is a bit frustrating.

The knock back. Zeds knock you around constantly, if you get overwhelmed. This almost always results in being pushed into a piece of geometry with a hitbox ten times the size of its model, getting you cornered/stuck. Leading to a quick death.

Lastly, less of a full complaint, and more of a warning, and that's the DLC. There's plenty of paid cosmetics, but there's also a fair amount of weaponry that's now locked behind a paywall. Normally, I wouldn't mind this type of thing, but the asking price for the weapons is ridiculous. $10~ for one single weapon is a bit much. Thankfully, you can buy the pass' which grant you all the paid weapons for much cheaper.

[h1]Conclusion (Pros & Cons)[/h1]  
Killing Floor 2 is well worth the investment for those that love wave based shooters. The game's bloody, detailed, and has loads to play around with.

[b]Pros:[/b]
+Dynamic survival
+High variety
+Endless
+Co-Op & Solo friendly
+Steam achievements

[b]Neutral:[/b]
~DLC weapons

[b]Cons:[/b]
-Enemy spawn behavior

[h1]Disclaimer[/h1]
Thank you for taking the time to read my review. Please remember, that this is just my opinion. We don't have to agree, one thing I love you may hate, and one thing I hate you may love. Hopefully this gave you an idea of what to expect. 

[b]Review lengths have been greatly compressed due to the character limit.[/b]

04/16/2026

Still one of the main games on Steam with a great soundtrack, engaging gameplay, and a very active player base even in the current year.

And so one of my favorite games has come to life again with new colors.
After the release of the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sequel, the game blossomed and smelled again. It's extremely a shame that the developers will no longer be bringing in new content, because they need to add a ton of crutches to their new creation with monetization like in a mobile game. So while the servers haven't been shut down yet, I strongly recommend everyone to try out this little game. It's ridiculously cheap on sale. In short, dopamine is guaranteed.

P.s don't forget to turn on vocals in the settings, there's really something to listen to there.

Killing Floor 2 is like if COD Zombies and Left 4 Dead had a baby. The gunplay is smooth, so much base content, so much fun with other people. Very good progression system, very rewarding. Highly Tuffing Recommend.

I bought this game many years ago (Ultimate Edition), recently installed it, but didn't receive the items. In 2026, this shouldn't be a problem, but here we are. It's better to just buy KF1 for a much lower price and get more enjoyment in the long run; there are many more mods compared to KF2.

The game constantly and severely lags even after configuring the files and playing on the lowest settings. My PC, of course, isn't super powerful, but it exceeds the recommended system requirements and should run the game without issues.
It's worth the money. It's a lot of fun, and if you get a full lobby of friends, it's even more fun. I will recommend this game every day.

The ultimate gore-fest. A perfect sequel that cranks everything up to eleven!

Killing Floor 2 took the brilliant foundation of the first game and turned it into the ultimate modern wave-based shooter. The first thing that blows you away is the "M.E.A.T." system—the gore and dismemberment in this game are incredibly detailed and easily the best in the industry. As the match progresses, the maps literally get painted red with blood, and it looks spectacular.

The combat feels phenomenal. The weapon animations are captured at a super high framerate, making the gunplay feel buttery smooth, punchy, and highly responsive. Melee combat also got a massive upgrade, allowing for satisfying parries and directional slashes. The perk system is much more versatile now, giving you meaningful choices every five levels to suit your specific playstyle.

The graphics are sharp, the Zed designs are terrifyingly detailed, and the heavy metal soundtrack (featuring bands like Demon Hunter and Impending Doom) pumps you up perfectly for the chaos. If you want a fast-paced, highly replayable co-op shooter with endless action and unmatched gunplay, Killing Floor 2 is pure perfection.
I love KF2, I've poured a couple thousand hours on Xbox and it's easily a 10/10 for me, I just wish more people still played it as it's a little hard to get into a populated match sometimes.

Solo play review:
All maps are well designed and some of the maps are exceptional, it is like fighting Zombies across the multiverse. The weapon variety is decent. Visual and audio effects are significanlty superior to Killing Floor 1 from 2009. It is worth the 94 GB space requirement. It is very convenient to jump into this game for  10-15 minutes, finish a map and then quit the game and back to your life. 

The game runs very smooth even on my low end hardware. The game crashed only once in my 12+ hours of gameplay (first map, wave 3 within 10 minute of gameplay). It is intended to be a multi player game yet my experience in Solo was very satisfactory. I can only imagine the fun with multi player but I don't have such friends. Highly recommended on Sale.

you know guys, ive been playing this game since release across multiple accounts and you still haven't fixed the whole skin reset bug on restarting your game, why... i love this game, you charge money for skins, theres awesome weekly events that reward awesome cosmetics but why, every single player game ive played that allowed for single player skins like these and even online games with similar mechanics never have this issue, this is a killing floor 2 exclusive bug.... it is genuinely disappointing that some of the smartest devs in the scene cant or won't fix this, just why man... disappointing, almost makes me not want to login and do the events you guys have crafted, this is genuinely an amazing killing floor game but you can't get that one detail right.

---for those that don't know, on restart ALL of your weapon skins are unequipped---

all of that being said im still gonna recommend this game to friends because killing floor 1 and 2 are fantastic titles, please fix this one bug and your players will give it a 12\10 like it deserves.

12 hordes of Creepers, appearing from around the nearest corner, thereby preventing escape from the crowd, out of 10.
So, I fly in with a hammer, everything explodes, blood, pieces of mutants, and the baseball player with Megaryam run at me, well, I just punched them in the face.

I used to play this game back in the day with my friend, but I definitely had nostalgia glasses on. Now, the game feels clunky. Running feels awful, which is probably tied to the weight system. I don't know who thought it would be smart to make it so you cannot outrun anyone in a wave based survival game. It doesn't even feel like you are moving faster when you run. Even the first Killing Floor had better-feeling movement. Honestly, if you want to play a round based zombie survival game, even Black Ops 1 and World at War are better than this.

Theres not much game here. Only one game mode is ever populated and while it can be fun the novelty runs out after a few matches. The environments are very well done and varied on a positive note and the bosses are fun to fight. That said theres just not much game here to justify buying it. If its on sale for $5 or less then get it but otherwise move on.

Killing Floor 2 — that's when you and your friends just wanted to calmly shoot some zombies, but ended up turning into a screaming gang of psychos who swear at each other between waves.
I stood with a shotgun, yelled "HOLD THEM!" and got shot in the back by my own teammate. Every match is a circus of shooting, screaming, and one genius who always buys the most expensive perk and immediately gets wiped out. A game where friendship is tested not by words, but by who accidentally blew up whom with a grenade. 9 out of 10, blood, sweat, and swearing — a perfect evening.

Does not support Widescreen and will only adjust FOV to a small fraction. I can only play the game if I play it though a keyhole. Upgraded my monitor and the only game in the past few years that does not support my UWHD

This game is a hard one to review. Not sure if i would recommend it or not.

Played for 106 hours way back before coming back to it, so i must of liked it. However.
There are some flaws here. 

1. Sprinting is not a thing, There is a sprint animation and your character does move faster, but only like 1-2% faster. So i can hardly call it a run not even a jog.

2. Hit detection / hitboxes and hit registration is not the greatest. You can aim down sight on an enemy and it will just go straight through them without dealing any damage whatsoever.
It is also quite easy to get blocked by either enemies or objects. 

3. Fall damage from small drops. For some reason you take fall damage from heights that should not hurt you,  Drop down one flight of stairs and you lose 10hp.

4. Enemies will both spawn and teleport to you. Yes this is one of those games were enemies will just spawn on you or right next to you. They will also teleport, so if you "run" one way, the enemies that were behind you will now magiaclly be up ahead. Obviously really annoying.

5. Creating your own lobby is super wonky. For god knows what reason, it will try to find an online match.
It makes absolutely no sense and makes it harder to make a private games with your friends.

6. Bullet time is incredibly annoying. It is clearly a relic of the past and a poorly implementet one at that. Bullet time gives you zero advantages and there is no cooldown between them so you can chain them.

I swear bullet time slows you down more than the enemies. Also if you were unlucky enough to start reloading when bullet time starts, then you are pretty much stuck in a froozen animation, because that is how slow reloading is in Bullet time. It is that slow that it feels like you are froozen.

7. Melee sucks. There is a melee class and i do not know why because they are useless. Enemies attacks faster than you and using your syringe to heal yourself seems to be slower as stowing your melee weapon for some reason takes longer than a gun.

You are weak as a wet noodle and the supposed lifesteal perk is not doing it. 

This game is fun and worth it (For the right price) But with the slugish movement and the teleporting enemies, frustration is guaranteed.

[h1]Amazing PVE co-op shooter with awesome music[/h1]

 The game has some unique vibe like music drive gameplay from Doom but with L4D fun with friends and unlike Killing Floor 3 the devs actually put a lot of effort to weld it together nicely

[b] PROS: [/b]
[list]
    [*]Music
    [*]Variety of maps
    [*]Interesting mechanics
    [*]Unique bosses
    [*]Atmosphere 
    [*]Graphics and physics for its time
    [*]Running good
    [*]A lot of different classes and perks
[/list]

[b] CONS: [/b]
[list]
     [*]Uneven difficulty in some areas

[/list]

Overall - 7.5/10.

Its a Decent Game Gameplay wise, nothing Special tho. Speaking in Metaphors, its like a Nice Sandwich, always Good for a quick Snack but no 5 Star Meal by a Looong Shot.

It has Microtransaction Shops and Lootboxes, but they are only Cosmetic, hence that Bull5hit is easily ignored.

I& dont play this Game often and when we do its always in a "Second monitor Gameplay" sorta way, to keep the Restless Hands Busy when Watching Something, hence why I& mostly play offline to enable the comfortable Pausing of the senseless Zombie Slaughter.

The Classes exist and take quite a bit to Max out. They Work alright and give mostly Prcentage Buffs to the Base Combatant fitting for the Given Classes Field, The 10 Perks of each Class (yes Im calling them that Cause Killing Floor 2's Naming about that is utterly Stupid) are giving more or less Good Boosts (pls note that there are 2 Perks per Tier and you can only Choose one per Tier, but Switch freely even in Match). 

Thats all its not a Great Game but its alright.
However, its the Perfect "Nothing Game" or "Timewaster Game" for me& when Watching something to Kill Zombies on the Side.

- Weird mobs and bosses design or I just don't have enough time to experience them all yet. I might get back to this comment if I changed my mind.

By death, death; by passions, Passion…

Disregarding the transgressions of youth and surpassing sins, I saved
My servants who died before, together with My chosen ones

Wondrous am I in My saints, I am Israel
By death, death; by passions, Passion

Having imitated the honorable, all martyrs
Received divine and blessed life
